data.title: Huili Resources Profit Slumps as Revenue Halves and One-Off Gains Fade
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Huili Resources (Group) Limited ( (HK:1303) ) has shared an announcement.

Huili Resources (Group) Limited reported a sharp decline in revenue to RMB2.26 billion for 2025 from RMB4.03 billion a year earlier, with gross profit falling to RMB132.5 million. The group’s profit from continuing operations shrank significantly, as higher administrative costs, increased expected credit losses on financial assets and net other losses drove operating profit down to RMB17.9 million from RMB173.8 million.

Net profit attributable to equity owners dropped to RMB11.6 million in 2025 from RMB160.3 million in 2024, reflecting the absence of contributions from discontinued operations and prior-year one-off gains. While net finance income turned positive, it was not enough to offset weaker operating performance, highlighting pressure on margins and earnings that may affect shareholder returns and signal a more challenging operating environment for the company.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1303) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.23 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Huili Resources (Group) Limited stock, see the HK:1303 Stock Forecast page.

More about Huili Resources (Group) Limited

Huili Resources (Group) Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, operating in the resources sector. The group generates revenue primarily from the sale of its resource-related products, positioning itself within the broader commodities and mining supply chain in mainland China and surrounding markets.

Average Trading Volume: 226,951

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$536.3M

data.title: Technovator Revamps Board Leadership With New Chairman and Non-Executive Director
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Technovator International ( (HK:1206) ) has issued an update.

Technovator International announced boardroom changes effective 27 March 2026, with chairman and non-executive director Li Chengfu and non-executive director Zhang Yanhua resigning from the board and its committees due to work arrangement adjustments, with both stating they had no disagreement with the board. The company has appointed Han Tao as non-executive director, board chairman, committee member and authorised representative, and Gao Peifeng as non-executive director and risk committee member, signaling a refresh of its governance structure and potential strategic influence from Han’s management experience at Tongfang-linked entities and state-backed investment firms.

Han Tao, a senior engineer with degrees in nuclear engineering from Tsinghua University and more than a decade of corporate management and project administration experience, has signed a one-year, automatically renewable service contract with no initial director fee, subject to review and shareholder-authorised board adjustments. His lack of other listed directorships, roles within the group, or ties to major shareholders underscores an independent profile within the non-executive ranks, while the appointments collectively aim to maintain regulatory compliance and continuity in Technovator’s leadership amid the transitions.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1206) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Technovator International stock, see the HK:1206 Stock Forecast page.

More about Technovator International

Technovator International Limited is a Singapore-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong that operates through a group structure, though the announcement focuses primarily on its board and governance. The group is overseen by a board of directors and various committees, including nomination, remuneration and risk management, reflecting a standard listed-company governance framework and regulatory compliance focus.

Average Trading Volume: 370,509

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$226.8M

data.title: Shandong Hi-Speed Holdings Profit Slumps in 2025 Amid Higher Costs and Losses
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest announcement is out from Shandong Hi-Speed Holdings Group Limited ( (HK:0412) ).

Shandong Hi-Speed Holdings Group Limited reported revenue of RMB5.03 billion for 2025, down from RMB5.58 billion a year earlier, with gross profit also declining. Despite strong fair value gains on financial assets and higher contributions from associates, rising other losses, increased impairment charges, and substantial finance costs weighed on performance.

Profit before tax fell sharply to RMB370.1 million from RMB794.5 million, while profit for the year dropped to RMB142.2 million, with a notable swing to a loss attributable to ordinary shareholders of RMB537.3 million and basic loss per share of RMB8.93 cents. Returns to holders of perpetual capital instruments and non-controlling interests remained positive, underscoring that ordinary equity holders bore the brunt of the earnings deterioration, which may pressure the group’s capital structure and investor sentiment.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0412) stock is a Hold with a HK$1.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Shandong Hi-Speed Holdings Group Limited stock, see the HK:0412 Stock Forecast page.

More about Shandong Hi-Speed Holdings Group Limited

Shandong Hi-Speed Holdings Group Limited, incorporated in Bermuda and listed in Hong Kong, operates as an investment holding company with a focus on infrastructure-related and financial investment businesses. The group is involved in providing diversified financial services and managing infrastructure assets, positioning itself as a key player in China’s broader infrastructure and investment sector.

Average Trading Volume: 12,421,387

Lai Si Enterprise Holding Ltd. ( (HK:2266) ) has provided an update.

Lai Si Enterprise Holding Limited has declared a final ordinary cash dividend of HKD 0.0146 per share for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, payable in Hong Kong dollars. The dividend is subject to shareholder approval on 23 June 2026, with an ex-dividend date of 25 June, a record date of 3 July, and payment scheduled for 17 July 2026.

The announcement confirms there is no applicable withholding tax on this dividend and no impact from listed warrants or convertible securities. The timetable gives investors clarity on entitlement and cash flow, underlining the company’s commitment to returning capital to shareholders and maintaining transparent corporate governance through its disclosed board composition.

data.title: SES S.A. Cuts Hybrid Debt by €327 Million in March Tender Offer
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An announcement from SES S.A. ( (FR:SESG) ) is now available.

On 26 March 2026, SES S.A. announced the results of a cash tender offer for its €625 million deeply subordinated fixed rate resettable securities, originally launched on 11 March 2026 and extended to an expiration deadline of 25 March. The company operates in the satellite communications sector and actively manages its hybrid capital to optimise its balance sheet and funding costs.

SES received valid tenders totaling €326.984 million in principal, set the final acceptance amount at that level, and confirmed that all tendered notes will be purchased in full at 99.25% of principal plus accrued interest, with settlement expected on 27 March 2026. Following cancellation of the repurchased securities, €198.038 million will remain outstanding, indicating a significant reduction in this hybrid layer of SES’s capital structure and potentially lowering future interest expense while simplifying its debt profile for investors.

The most recent analyst rating on (FR:SESG) stock is a Hold with a EUR5.85 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on SES S.A. stock, see the FR:SESG Stock Forecast page.

More about SES S.A.

SES S.A., based in Luxembourg, operates in the global satellite communications industry, providing satellite-based video and data connectivity services to broadcasters, governments, telecom operators and enterprise customers. The company issues and manages complex debt instruments, including deeply subordinated hybrid securities, as part of its capital structure and funding strategy.

Average Trading Volume: 579,917

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: €2.55B

data.title: Clarke to Acquire Debt-Strained Ravelin Properties REIT in $1.1 Billion Deal
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An update from Clarke Inc. ( (TSE:CKI) ) is now available.

Clarke Inc. has agreed to acquire all outstanding units and convertible debentures of Ravelin Properties REIT in a court-approved plan of arrangement that values the REIT at $1.1 billion, including debt, and the combined entity at $1.7 billion. The all-share transaction will see Ravelin unitholders and debentureholders receive Clarke shares, including a substantial premium for the REIT debentures, and is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026, subject to regulatory and securityholder approvals.

The deal is positioned as a recapitalization solution for Ravelin, whose board had been exploring options amid ongoing debt defaults and capital needs, and will shift Ravelin securityholders to a 16.2% stake in a larger, more diversified Clarke-led platform. Clarke argues the combination will ease Ravelin’s liquidity and lender pressures, allow management to refocus on improving occupancy and portfolio performance, and enhance scale and geographic diversification for Clarke shareholders, while G2S2 Capital’s extended forbearance provides a backstop if debentureholder or meeting approvals are not secured.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:CKI) stock is a Hold with a C$23.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Clarke Inc. stock, see the TSE:CKI Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on CKI Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CKI is a Neutral.

The score is primarily held back by mixed financial quality—especially weak and inconsistent free cash flow and increased leverage—despite solid recent profitability. Technical signals are also soft with negative momentum and the price below key moving averages. Valuation appears mid-range based on the P/E, offering limited offset to the financial and trend risks.

To see Spark’s full report on CKI stock, click here.

More about Clarke Inc.

Clarke Inc. is a Toronto Stock Exchange-listed investment company with a focus on acquiring and managing businesses and assets, including real estate, to create long-term value for shareholders. Ravelin Properties REIT is a TSX-listed real estate investment trust that owns and operates a portfolio of income-producing properties and has been facing significant financial and liquidity pressures tied to high leverage and debt defaults.

Current Market Cap: C$301.5M

data.title: Hudbay Extends Mine Lives and Lifts Copper Outlook in New Three-Year Guidance
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest announcement is out from Hudbay Minerals ( (TSE:HBM) ).

Hudbay Minerals has updated its mineral reserves and resources and issued new production guidance through 2028, outlining a significant uplift in copper and gold output from its core Americas portfolio. The company expects consolidated copper production to average 147,000 tonnes annually over the next three years, rising to 159,000 tonnes in 2027–2028, while gold production is projected to average 243,000 ounces, supported by optimization projects and new ore sources.

Mine life has been extended at all three major operations, with Constancia now expected to run to 2040, Snow Lake to 2041 and Copper Mountain to 2045, underpinned by higher mill throughput and improved grades. Hudbay is also advancing a large exploration program in Snow Lake and progressing the Copper World project toward a 2026 sanctioning decision, bolstered by a $600 million joint venture with Mitsubishi, positioning the company for higher long-term copper exposure and enhanced growth potential for stakeholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:HBM) stock is a Hold with a C$30.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Hudbay Minerals stock, see the TSE:HBM Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on HBM Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, HBM is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by improved fundamentals (earnings rebound and stronger leverage profile) and a constructive earnings-call outlook with strengthened liquidity, partially offset by weak technical momentum and uneven/volatile free cash flow conversion. Valuation is supportive due to a moderate P/E and very high dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on HBM stock, click here.

More about Hudbay Minerals

Hudbay Minerals is a Canadian mining company focused on copper and gold production from three long-life operations in tier-one jurisdictions across the Americas. Its key assets include the Constancia mine in Peru, the Snow Lake operations in Manitoba and the Copper Mountain mine in British Columbia, with additional growth potential from the Copper World project in Arizona and other brownfield and exploration initiatives.
